beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home, check on houseplants
beats.reasoning: Monday morning wake-up with full energy; Mia starts her day grounded at home, tending to her plants and having her own coffee before heading to work.
Drive from Rainier Valley to Ballard (roughly 20–25 min via MLK to I-5 N); Light Rail closure 9am–3pm is irrelevant for this car commute but noted as context.

beats.activity: Morning shift as barista and shift lead; prep, service, training
beats.reasoning: Mia opens her shift and runs the morning service; the 11am FIFA match is happening, but as a shift lead she's focused on work, not watching—this is not her priority.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab a quick sandwich and walk by the water
beats.reasoning: Beautiful sunny Monday; Mia takes a proper lunch break outdoors, enjoying the weather and a neighborhood walk instead of being glued to the FIFA match.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift; espresso prep, customer service, closing tasks
beats.reasoning: Mia completes her shift lead duties, managing the afternoon rush and prep for the evening staff.
Return drive to Rainier Valley; Light Rail closure now ending (3pm) but Mia is driving anyway, so no impact.

beats.activity: Errand: grocery run for the week; pick up fresh produce and supplies
beats.reasoning: After work, Mia shops for the week while the weather is still beautiful; she's community-minded and values fresh, local ingredients for her meals.
Short drive home from Safeway to unload and settle in.

beats.activity: Paint in home studio; work on a landscape inspired by fly-fishing trips
beats.reasoning: Sunny day and good energy; Mia dedicates evening time to her painting passion, relaxing at home before winding down.

beats.activity: Prepare and enjoy dinner; light conversation with a close friend over video call
beats.reasoning: Mia values time with close friends; a relaxed dinner and catch-up call fits her community-minded nature and evening routine.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: tea, read, tend to houseplants
beats.reasoning: Mia settles into a quiet evening routine, checking on her named plants and reading before bed.
Mia gets a solid 7h 45m sleep after a full, satisfying day of work, outdoor time, and creative pursuits.

diary: Monday started early with my usual ritual—coffee at home, checking on the ferns. The commute to Ballard was smooth, thank god; I heard the Light Rail was down all morning. The shop was buzzing from open, everyone hyped about the World Cup match at 11. I trained one of our newer baristas on the Nuova Simonelli while fielding orders—kept things moving. Grabbed a sandwich at lunch and walked down by the water for twenty minutes, just needed the quiet. Afternoon flew by, espresso shots and regulars asking about the USA-Wales game. Got home around two, did my weekly Safeway run, then spent an hour in the studio working on that fly-fishing landscape I've been nursing. The light was perfect through the window. Dinner video call with Claire was exactly what I needed—we laughed about old times. Ended the day with tea and the plants, feeling settled.
thought_process: The World Cup buzz had me expecting chaos, but Ballard handled it well—maybe people were still at work or waiting for evening viewing. I chose to keep my lunch break short and grounded, sensing I'd need that quiet by the water to reset before the afternoon rush. Training the new hire felt important today; she's got potential. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me directly since I bike-commute, but it made me grateful for my route. After work, I deliberately carved out studio time instead of collapsing—the fishing landscape has been calling me all week. Dinner with Claire reminded me why I need those check-ins; the day had been pleasant but steady, and hearing her voice shifted something in me from 'got through it' to actually content.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
